name: Avery Parrish
abstract: James Avery Parrish (January 24, 1917 – December 10, 1959) was an American jazz pianist, composer and arranger. He wrote and recorded "After Hours".

about_quotes.speaker: Erskine Hawkins
about_quotes.evidence: I showed Avery how to arrange... he did a lot of writing for me also... composed by Avery Parrish

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Leonard Feather had hired Big Sid Catlett and I to do a record date during the band to accompany Linda Keene, a Dixieland Blues singer and Avery Parrish was the piano player.

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Do you remember the piano player that was with Erskine Hawkins, Avery Parrish? Oh, yes, Avery. Oh, yes. Sure, Avery, yeah.

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Bryant
about_quotes.evidence: I learned it from the original recording by Erskine Hawkins, featuring Avery Parrish at the piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Bryant interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I knew Sammy Lowe and Dud Bascomb and Jimmy Mitchell and Avery Parrish. I knew all of these guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995
